question:
[Previous Feedback] v2 data cleaned successfully. Output contains the channel column and all expected fields.

Let's capture what's still on our plate post-update-1 in a structured doc the schema validator will actually accept — `project/docs/action_items_u1.json`. Our intake tool expects the `action_items` schema, which looks like this:

```json
{
  "update": "update1",
  "action_items": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "owner": "string",
      "status": "not_started|in_progress|blocked|completed|cancelled|deferred",
      "due": "YYYY-MM-DD"
    }
  ]
}
```

At least four real items — what's actually open after the schema clarification, not filler. Owners should be one of the four of us; anything we've already shipped doesn't belong in here, it just confuses the next standup. Statuses must come from the enum above (the validator rejects anything else).

answer.text: Done, created and validated `project/docs/action_items_u1.json` with six open post-update-1 items, all using allowed statuses and date format.

Statuses must come from the enum above (the validator rejects anything else).

arguments.command:
python - <<'PY'
import json, re
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('project/docs/action_items_u1.json')
data=json.loads(p.read_text())
allowed={'not_started','in_progress','blocked','completed','cancelled','deferred'}
owners={'Alex','Maya','Jordan','Sam'}
assert data['update']=='update1'
assert len(data['action_items'])>=4
for item in data['action_items']:
    assert set(item)=={'id','description','owner','status','due'}, item
    assert item['owner'] in owners, item
    assert item['status'] in allowed, item
    assert re.fullmatch(r'\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}', item['due']), item
print('valid action_items_u1.json')
print(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
PY

{"command":"python - <<'PY'
import json, re
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('project/docs/action_items_u1.json')
data=json.loads(p.read_text())
allowed={'not_started','in_progress','blocked','completed','cancelled','deferred'}
owners={'Alex','Maya','Jordan','Sam'}
assert data['update']=='update1'
assert len(data['action_items'])>=4
for item in data['action_items']:
    assert set(item)=={'id','description','owner','status','due'}, item
    assert item['owner'] in owners, item
    assert item['status'] in allowed, item
    assert re.fullmatch(r'\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}', item['due']), item
print('valid action_items_u1.json')
print(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
